name: stable
statement_text:
A general linear method (A, U, B, V ) is ‘stable’ if there
exists a constant C such that, for all n = 1, 2, . . . , V n ≤ C.

variables.name: V
variables.definition: V is the matrix from the general linear method (A, U, B, V)

variables.name: A
variables.definition: A is one of the four defining matrices of a general linear method

variables.name: U
variables.definition: U is one of the four defining matrices of a general linear method

variables.name: B
variables.definition: B is one of the four defining matrices of a general linear method

variables.name: u
variables.definition: preconsistency vector satisfying V u = u and U u = 1

variables.name: v
variables.definition: vector such that B1 + V v = u + v for a consistent method

preamble: The text discusses general linear methods defined by four matrices (A, U, B, V). Preconsistency and consistency conditions are defined using vectors u and v. Stability is defined in terms of the power-boundedness of the matrix V.

dependencies.name: stability in the sense of Dahlquist
